Lyrics are overall pretty solid on this one but I will mention that I don’t like when lyrics include a specific number of unknown significance like “twenty-three”
I feel like this should be higher but it’s kind of basic. Maybe I’m wrong.
Vocals don't blend very well and the lyrics are kind of all over the place even when they aren't just repeating the main line.
Lyrics aren't bad but again I wish there was more variance in the second half.
Lyrics aren't bad but again I wish there was more variance in the second half.
The scraping noise on the right side is a bit odd towards the beginning. I love how faithfully the instrumental follows the vocals through the different sections. Chorus is a bit repetitive.
I feel like the second half could have blended much better but the background vocals frequently started and stopped instead of being sustained.
logs.songName: Somebody That I Used To Know

logs.comments: I don't like the heavily autotuned section but aside from that he's clearly a good singer.
I don't like the heavily autotuned section but aside from that he's clearly a good singer.
The repeating chorus makes up far too much of the song in my opinion
The vocals, particularly during the bridge, are excellent. The chorus dominates too much of the song but I didn't lower the score for now.
The song builds well
The vocal score feels slightly too high and the background score slightly too low so I figure it kind of balances out
This is a hard one. I think vocals, background and cohesion all could debatably be increased
This was a pretty confusing one to grade. The instrumental and vocals have a lot of raw emotion. The pre chorus and chorus still make up too much of the song.
Once again the chorus takes up so much of the song. I also just don't feel like the song has an overall direction
No 10 on vocals because he (understandably) does not sound completely natural in every register. Although chorus dominates lyrics, it's sung differently every time
That voice is unbelievable, especially at the end after the key change. So hauntingly beautiful. No clear problems with any other part of the song
That voice is unbelievable, especially at the end after the key change. So hauntingly beautiful. No clear problems with any other part of the song. I might actually prefer the live version though
There's a better version on youtube. His pronunciation is much better there and his vocals blend much better. I'd give that one a 10 in vocals and not dock on cohesion. The song builds well though.
logs.songName: S.O.S D'un Terrien en detresse - Live

logs.comments: A good example of how even a small change to a line in the chorus can improve the lyrics. Even so I could see the lyrics and flow being lower
I like how the song is clearly moving towards the climax and then transitions into an outro afterwards
I love the little drum fills. I'll keep saying it though, still too much chorus on all of these songs with no variation. It was a pretty good transition out of the chorus into the ending though
The bridge was what initially sold me on this song, it's very nice. The variation on the chorus at the end is also very well done. I might even consider upping the vocal score on this one
I think the added vocal accents save the flow of this song in the last chorus. I really wish more songs did this
Bridge is a little odd but it recovers well into the next section

There are a lot of things I like and don't like about this song. The lyrics in the first verse feel a bit conflicting with some being poignant and others a bit crass. The vocals are nice aside from the autotune
Although the repeated sections are executed different, there are essentially no unique lyrics outside of them
The vocals are very impressive. I took a bit off the flow because in the second half the song kind of devolves into chaos. Good sounding chaos, but still nonsensical
Slightly repetitive chorus
Although the repeated sections are executed different, there are essentially no unique lyrics outside of them
I feel that during the climax of the song the instrumental gets so overwhelming that it takes away the solemn vibe from the start of the song
I think just repeating the chorus again is a very boring way to end the song and overall the song isn't very interesting after the second verse
The song cuts off very suddenly. I definitely think you could justify raising the background score
I like the theme of the lyrics being advice. There are a lot of unique lines.
